channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Trump supports Musk’s idea to send some DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: President Donald Trump said that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend. This comes as labor unions, Democrats and federal employees have filed several lawsuits against DOGE.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: David Boren, a former Oklahoma governor and veteran US senator and university president, dies at 83
item.description: David Boren, the former Oklahoma governor and longtime U.S. Senate intelligence chief who went on to lead the University of Oklahoma after his retirement from politics, has died.
item.dc:creator: Tim Talley And Sean Murphy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Reputed migrant gang members busted in NYC drug, gun raid but likely to avoid prosecution
item.description: Two suspected members of the violent Tren de Aragua (TdA) gang were busted in a gun and drug raid in a Big Apple auto repair shop earlier this month – and have since been cut loose on lesser charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Feds pause contract providing legal services to thousands of unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – An El Paso nonprofit plans to sue the federal government for halting their ability to provide legal services to migrant minors in federal custody.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds rally against firing of federal workers, emphasizing threats to public health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hundreds of current and former federal employees rallied Wednesday outside the Department of Health and Human Services headquarters, protesting recent firings and their effect on public health, and directing much of their ire at Elon Musk and his agency's efforts to cut spending.
item.dc:creator: NATHAN ELLGREN and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cynthia Erivo to host 78th Annual Tony Awards
item.description: "Wicked" actress Cynthia Erivo is set to host the 78th Annual Tony Awards. “I am so proud and excited to take on this glorious honor,” said Erivo.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal government pulls approval for NYC congestion pricing
item.description: "New York State’s congestion pricing plan is a slap in the face to working class Americans and small business owners,” Secretary of Transportation Sean Duffy said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Man dies after first responders dismiss 911 call as 'unfounded': Sources
item.description: The EMS lieutenant called 911 during an apparent asthma attack around noon at his home on East 73rd Street in Brooklyn, sources said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk finally reveals 'why Memphis' for xAI supercomputer
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Months after xAI put its operations in Memphis, Elon Musk finally announced why he chose the Bluff City to host the world's largest artificial intelligence supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Woman pulled from tight storm drain in California
item.description: The woman, who was "found unclothed above the waist," was taken to a local hospital for evaluation, though she "had no obvious injuries.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin police department in 'critical need' for more officers
item.description: The Whitewater Police Department said in a Facebook post that they haven't seen an increase in police staffing for 17 years, resulting in the 'critical need' for more officers.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, NewsNation

item.title: Cal Fire captain stabbed, killed in her home
item.description: A Cal Fire captain was fatally stabbed at her home in Ramona, California, Monday in what San Diego County Sheriff's Department officials are investigating as a suspected homicide.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione speaks out for first time, sends message to supporters
item.description: "I am overwhelmed by - and grateful for - everyone who has written me to share their stories and express their support," Luigi Mangione wrote.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bosar, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. military tests unarmed nuclear missile in Southern California
item.description: If you were outside around 1 a.m. Wednesday on the West Coast, you may have seen or heard the U.S. military test-launching an intercontinental ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dramatic dashcam footage shows moment Sacramento driver flies off busy overpass: video
item.description: Dashcam footage captured the dramatic moment when a vehicle flew off the Interstate 5 overpass and dropped on to a busy Sacramento freeway on Presidents' Day, injuring only the driver.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois governor to back 'screen-free schools' and join national trend to ban cellphones in class
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker is aiming to improve student achievement, social interaction and the mental health of public school students by proposing a statewide ban on cellphones in classrooms, an idea that is rapidly gaining traction nationally regardless of political persuasion.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Winter-weary East Coast hit with another storm as temperatures plunge elsewhere
item.description: Officials have urged residents to stay off the roads in portions of Virginia and North Carolina, where a storm dropped heavy snow and caused hundreds of accidents in places that aren’t accustomed to significant accumulations.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: Southwest Airlines cutting hundreds of jobs
item.description: Southwest Airlines announced Monday that it will cut 1,750 jobs in late April in what will be the first round of layoffs in the company’s 53-year history.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: SUMTERVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Native American activist Leonard Peltier was released from a Florida prison on Tuesday, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utahns participate in nationwide Presidents' Day protests against Trump, Musk
item.description: Hundreds of Utahns gathered at the State Capitol on Monday, Feb. 17, to participate in an hours-long protest against President Donald Trump — but not everyone at the Capitol shared the same viewpoint.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
